description: Phase 2 — Write operations (PLAN.md §9; ADR-0003 mutations + authenticated network ops go through the shell-out git tier; ADR-0006 reuse system git credential helpers / ssh-agent). Adds the mutating daily-driver surface on top of Phase 1's read-only foundation: working-tree status, stage/unstage (whole-file then line/hunk partial), discard, commit/amend, branches/tags, checkout, fetch/pull/push with credentials, stash, merge, cherry-pick, revert, and drag-&-drop merge/rebase. EXIT: Lady is a daily-driver for common git workflows as well as Fork. Interactive rebase, the 3-pane conflict resolver, signing, worktrees, reflog, and bisect are Phase 3 (NOT here). No AI (Phase 5). Branches from ralph/phase-1-read-only (Phase 1 complete). Each story = one context-window commit. After any mutation the UI must refresh status + refs + graph so views never go stale (PLAN.md §3.2). UI stories rely on build + typecheck + unit tests for loop verification; a manual window check is noted in progress.txt since Tauri windows cannot be verified headless. Mutating engine methods MUST be unit-tested against throwaway tempdir repos (system git permitted for fixtures, ADR-0003).

userStories.title: Working-tree status model + Changes view
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see what has changed in my working tree, split into staged and unstaged.
acceptanceCriteria: lady-proto: WorkingTree { staged: Vec<FileStatus>, unstaged: Vec<FileStatus>, untracked: Vec<String> } and FileStatus { path, old_path: Option<String>, kind: ChangeKind } where ChangeKind = Added|Modified|Deleted|Renamed|Untracked|Conflicted; serde round-trip test
lady-git: GitEngine::status(repo) -> WorkingTree, parsing `git status --porcelain=v2 -z --untracked-files=all` via the shell-out tier (ADR-0003) for exact git semantics incl. renames and conflicts
Tauri command status(repo) -> WorkingTree; TS types mirror WorkingTree/FileStatus/ChangeKind
ui: a 'Changes' view (new tab) lists staged vs unstaged vs untracked files with a per-file change badge; empty state when the tree is clean

Unit test: tempdir repo with one staged, one unstaged-modified, and one untracked file yields the right buckets; cargo fmt/clippy -D warnings/test green; cargo deny passes; npm run build green; manual window note in progress.txt
userStories.notes: PLAN.md §4.1 status -> WorkingTree; §6.9 status refresh < ~100ms warm. Read via shell-out porcelain for exactness; gix status acceptable if it matches the test, but porcelain=v2 -z is the spec. Foundation for all staging stories.

userStories.title: Stage / unstage whole files
userStories.description: As a user, I want to stage and unstage entire files.
acceptanceCriteria: lady-git: stage_paths(repo, paths) (shell-out `git add -- <paths>`, handling deletes) and unstage_paths(repo, paths) (`git restore --staged -- <paths>`, or `git reset -q HEAD -- <paths>` on an unborn branch)
Tauri commands stage_paths / unstage_paths; the Changes view gets stage/unstage buttons per file and stage-all / unstage-all actions
After staging/unstaging the UI re-fetches status so the buckets update immediately (PLAN.md §3.2)
Unit test: stage then unstage a path moves it between buckets in status() output; cargo fmt/clippy -D warnings/test green; cargo deny passes; npm run build green; manual window note in progress.txt
userStories.notes: Mutations via shell-out git (ADR-0003). Handle add/modify/delete and the unborn-HEAD (no commits yet) edge.

userStories.title: Working & staged diffs in the diff viewer
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see the actual line changes for a working or staged file, not just its name.
acceptanceCriteria: lady-git: generalize diffing to a DiffSpec — Commit(oid) (existing), WorkingVsIndex(path) (unstaged), IndexVsHead(path) (staged); keep diff_commit working
Tauri command diff_spec(repo, spec) -> Vec<FileDiff>; TS DiffSpec mirror; the existing commit diff path keeps working
ui: selecting a file in the Changes view shows its diff in the Phase 1 DiffView (unstaged -> WorkingVsIndex, staged -> IndexVsHead), reusing side-by-side/unified + syntax highlight + image diff
Unit test on each DiffSpec variant against a tempdir repo; cargo fmt/clippy -D warnings/test green; cargo deny passes; npm run build green; manual window note in progress.txt
userStories.notes: PLAN.md §4.1 diff(repo, spec). Reuses Phase 1 DiffView/lady-diff. Working-vs-index = on-disk bytes vs index blob; index-vs-HEAD = index blob vs HEAD tree blob.

userStories.title: Partial staging — hunk level (patch builder)
userStories.description: As a user, I want to stage individual hunks instead of whole files.
acceptanceCriteria: lady-diff: a partial-stage patch builder that emits a valid unified diff for a chosen subset of hunks of one file (correct @@ headers + counts), suitable for `git apply --cached`
lady-git: apply_patch(repo, patch, reverse: bool) (shell-out `git apply --cached [--reverse] -`, patch on stdin) to stage (forward) or unstage (reverse) selected hunks
ui: per-hunk 'Stage hunk' / 'Unstage hunk' actions in the Changes/diff view that build the patch and apply it, then refresh status + diff

Unit test: build a patch for 1 of 2 hunks, apply --cached, assert only that hunk is staged (status + staged diff) and the index is not corrupted; cargo fmt/clippy -D warnings/test green; cargo deny passes; npm run build green; manual window note in progress.txt
userStories.notes: PLAN.md §6.3 — the hard one; foundation for AI Commit Composer later. PH1-007 deferred the patch builder to here. Test against real `git apply` to guarantee patch validity.

userStories.title: Partial staging — line level + discard
userStories.description: As a user, I want to stage or discard individual lines, not just hunks.
acceptanceCriteria: lady-diff: extend the patch builder to select arbitrary added/removed lines within a hunk (unselected changes become context), producing a valid applicable patch
lady-git: discard(repo, spec) to discard unstaged hunks/lines (reverse patch to the working tree via `git apply --reverse`, no --cached) and discard whole untracked files
ui: line-level selection in DiffView with 'Stage lines' / 'Discard lines' (and 'Discard hunk'); a confirm step before discard (destructive)

Unit test: stage a single added line out of several; discard a single line restores it; cargo fmt/clippy -D warnings/test green; cargo deny passes; npm run build green; manual window note in progress.txt
userStories.notes: PLAN.md §1 discard hunks/lines + §6.3. Discard is destructive — gate behind explicit confirm; never silently lose work.

userStories.title: Commit + amend
userStories.description: As a user, I want to commit the staged changes and amend the last commit.
acceptanceCriteria: lady-git: commit(repo, message, CommitOpts { amend: bool }) via shell-out git (`git commit -m` / `git commit --amend -m`); returns the new commit Oid
Recent commit-message access: recent_messages(repo, limit) -> Vec<String> from the log

ui: a commit box on the Changes view with a message field, Commit button (disabled when nothing staged), an Amend toggle that prefills the last message, and a recent-messages affordance; after commit the message clears and status + refs + graph refresh

Unit test: stage + commit creates a commit whose diff matches; amend rewrites the tip without adding a commit; cargo fmt/clippy -D warnings/test green; cargo deny passes; npm run build green; manual window note in progress.txt
userStories.notes: PLAN.md §1 commit/amend with recent-message access. Signing is NOT here (Phase 3) — leave the user's commit.gpgsign config untouched; do not force-disable it.

userStories.title: Branches + tags (create / delete / checkout)
userStories.description: As a user, I want to create, delete, and switch branches, and create/delete tags.
acceptanceCriteria: lady-git: create_branch(repo, name, start_point?), delete_branch(repo, name, force), checkout(repo, target) (branch or revision; detached-HEAD aware), create_tag(repo, name, target?, message?), delete_tag(repo, name) — all shell-out git
Tauri commands for each; the Refs view gets create/delete/checkout actions and a new-branch / new-tag affordance
checkout refuses with a clear error when the working tree would be overwritten (surface git's message; do not force unless the user opts in)
After any of these the UI refreshes refs + status + graph; current branch/HEAD is visibly indicated

Unit tests: create+checkout a branch moves HEAD; delete removes the ref; create+delete a tag; cargo fmt/clippy -D warnings/test green; cargo deny passes; npm run build green; manual window note in progress.txt
userStories.notes: PLAN.md §1 create/delete branches & tags, checkout. Drag-&-drop is PH2-012. Keep destructive flags (force delete, force checkout) explicit.

userStories.title: Fetch / pull / push with credentials + progress
userStories.description: As a user, I want to sync with my remote over the network using my existing git credentials.
acceptanceCriteria: lady-git: fetch(repo, remote?), pull(repo, remote?, branch?), push(repo, remote?, branch?, set_upstream: bool, force: bool) via shell-out git so the user's credential helpers + ssh-agent are reused untouched (ADR-0003, ADR-0006)
Progress: stream git's stderr progress lines to the frontend as events (reuse the clone-progress pattern from PH1-011), e.g. fetch-progress / push-progress

ui: Fetch / Pull / Push controls; an ahead/behind indicator for the current branch vs its upstream; push offers set-upstream when none exists; errors (auth failure, non-fast-forward) surface git's message verbatim
After a successful op the UI refreshes refs + graph + status

Test: a local 'remote' (a second tempdir / bare repo on disk, file:// or path) — push then fetch round-trips a commit; ahead/behind computed correctly; no real network in tests; cargo fmt/clippy -D warnings/test green; cargo deny passes; npm run build green; manual window note in progress.txt
userStories.notes: ADR-0006 — reuse system git auth; keyring holds only hosting-API tokens (not here). Never gate network ops behind anything; surface git errors faithfully. Test with file:// or path remotes to stay offline.

userStories.title: Stash (save / pop / apply / drop) + inline display
userStories.description: As a user, I want to stash changes and manage my stash list.
acceptanceCriteria: lady-git: stash_save(repo, message?, include_untracked: bool), stash_list(repo) -> Vec<StashEntry { index, message, oid }>, stash_apply(repo, index), stash_pop(repo, index), stash_drop(repo, index) — shell-out git; lady-proto StashEntry + serde test
Tauri commands for each; ui: a stash list with apply/pop/drop, and a Stash button on the Changes view (with an include-untracked option)
Stashes shown inline in the commit list (Fork parity) — render stash entries as distinct rows/markers alongside commits
After any stash op the UI refreshes status + the stash list + graph

Unit tests: save then list shows the entry; pop re-applies and removes it; drop removes without applying; cargo fmt/clippy -D warnings/test green; cargo deny passes; npm run build green; manual window note in progress.txt
userStories.notes: PLAN.md §1 stash + 'stashes shown inline in the commit list'. Inline graph marker may be a thin follow-up if heavy, but the list + ops are required.

userStories.title: Merge (fast-forward / no-ff) + abort
userStories.description: As a user, I want to merge a branch into my current branch.
acceptanceCriteria: lady-git: merge(repo, source, MergeOpts { fast_forward: FfMode (Auto|Only|Never), commit_message? }) and merge_abort(repo) via shell-out git
merge reports its result: AlreadyUpToDate | FastForwarded | Merged(oid) | Conflicts(Vec<path>); conflicts surfaced (full 3-pane resolver is Phase 3 — here just list conflicted files + offer abort)
ui: a Merge action (from a branch in the Refs view) with ff-mode choice; on conflict show conflicted files + an Abort button; on success refresh graph + refs + status

Unit tests: ff merge fast-forwards; divergent no-conflict merge creates a merge commit; conflicting merge reports Conflicts and merge_abort restores pre-merge state; cargo fmt/clippy -D warnings/test green; cargo deny passes; npm run build green; manual window note in progress.txt
userStories.notes: PLAN.md §1 merge (ff / no-ff). Conflict RESOLUTION (3-pane) is Phase 3; this story only detects + lists + aborts.

userStories.title: Cherry-pick + revert
userStories.description: As a user, I want to cherry-pick a commit onto my branch and revert a commit.
acceptanceCriteria: lady-git: cherry_pick(repo, oid) and revert(repo, oid) via shell-out git; both report Applied(oid) | Conflicts(Vec<path>); plus a shared sequencer-abort (cherry-pick/revert --abort)
ui: Cherry-pick and Revert actions from a commit's context in the graph/commit list; on conflict show conflicted files + Abort; on success refresh graph + status

Unit tests: cherry-pick a commit from another branch applies its change; revert a commit undoes its change; a conflicting cherry-pick reports Conflicts and abort restores state; cargo fmt/clippy -D warnings/test green; cargo deny passes; npm run build green; manual window note in progress.txt
userStories.notes: PLAN.md §1 cherry-pick, revert. Same conflict policy as merge: detect + list + abort; resolver is Phase 3.

userStories.title: Drag-&-drop merge / rebase (drag branch onto branch)
userStories.description: As a user, I want to drag one branch onto another to merge or rebase.
acceptanceCriteria: lady-git: rebase(repo, branch, onto) (plain, non-interactive) via shell-out git, reporting Rebased | Conflicts(Vec<path>) with rebase_abort; interactive rebase stays Phase 3
ui: drag a branch ref onto another branch ref (Refs/graph) opens a small choice — Merge or Rebase — then runs the chosen op via the existing merge/rebase commands
Conflicts surface conflicted files + Abort (resolver is Phase 3); on success refresh graph + refs + status

cargo fmt/clippy -D warnings/test green (unit-test plain rebase: linear rebase replays commits; conflicting rebase reports Conflicts + abort restores); cargo deny passes; npm run build green; manual window note in progress.txt

PHASE 2 EXIT: change, partial-stage, commit/amend, branch/tag, checkout, fetch/pull/push, stash, merge, cherry-pick, revert, and drag-&-drop merge/rebase all work in the window as well as Fork's daily-use surface. Note this in progress.txt. Next is a Phase 3 PRD (new branchName ralph/phase-3-advanced-ship).
userStories.notes: PLAN.md §1 drag-&-drop merge/rebase. Reuses PH2-010 merge + this story's plain rebase. Interactive rebase + 3-pane resolver + signing + worktrees/reflog/bisect are all Phase 3.
